findings.claim: redeem burns 18-decimal dreAmount but transfers the same raw number as 6-decimal USDC, so users can drain ~1e12x more USDC than they deposited (decimal mismatch breaks the peg).

route.rationale: The decimal mismatch lets redeem pay out far more value than deposited, exactly what check_redeemReturnsDeposit asserts against.

findings.claim: yield deposited before/at a single block lets an attacker deposit just before distribution and withdraw immediately after to capture rewards, defeating time-weighted accrual and enabling sandwich/donation theft.

route.rationale: Time-weighted accrual/sandwich timing is a temporal economic flaw with no matching invariant or arithmetic obligation, so it escalates to a human.

verification.note: no formal tool fit — escalated to human

findings.claim: return values of usdc.transferFrom are unchecked, so a non-reverting failed transfer mints unbacked dreUSD.

route.rationale: Unchecked transferFrom can mint dreUSD without backing collateral, which check_supplyAtMostBacking directly tests.

findings.claim: first-depositor/donation inflation attack via direct asset transfer can manipulate share price and steal subsequent depositors' funds.

route.rationale: Donation-based share-price inflation depends on external transfers and has no matching invariant; it is not a clean mulDiv rounding obligation, so escalate.

verification.note: no formal tool fit — escalated to human
